papers.title: Modified lignin in tobacco and poplar plants over-expressing the Arabidopsis gene encoding ferulate 5-hydroxylase
papers.authors: Meyermans H, Morreel K, Lapierre C, Pollet B, De Bruyn A, Busson R, Herdewijn P, Devreese B, Van Beeumen J, Marita JM, Ralph J, Chen C, Burggraeve B, Van Montagu M, Messens E, Boerjan W

papers.title: Significant increases in pulping efficiency in C4H-F5H-transformed poplars
papers.authors: Huntley SK, Ellis D, Gilbert M, Chapple C, Mansfield SD
papers.journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

edges.ev: F5H is a key enzyme involved in synthesizing the monolignol sinapyl alcohol and, ultimately, S lignin moieties
F5H affects the partitioning between the two major traditional monolignols, coniferyl and sinapyl alcohols
the final methylation step, catalyzed by caffeic acid 3-O-methyl transferase (COMT) produce sinapaldehyde and ultimately sinapyl alcohol
only coupling of a monolignol with a G unit can result in beta-5-coupled products
CAld5H catalyzes 5-hydroxylation of coniferyl aldehyde into 5-hydroxyconiferyl aldehyde
5-hydroxyconiferyl aldehyde was converted exclusively into sinapyl aldehyde when incubated with LsCOMT
kcat/Km of CAld5H for coniferyl aldehyde was approximately 140 times greater than for ferulate; coniferyl aldehyde is the preferred substrate
Ferulate-5-hydroxylase (F5H) is a key rate-limiting enzyme required for the biosynthesis of syringyl (S) monolignols
Line 64 exhibits ca. 94 mol%, line 82 exhibits ca. 85 mol%, and the wild type (WT) exhibits ca. 65 mol% of syringyl lignin units
PtrCAld5H1 and PtrCAld5H2 have essentially the same biochemical functions; 5-hydroxylation requires combined metabolic flux of these two CAld5Hs
CAld5H requires a cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR) as an electron donor
Overexpression of Arabidopsis F5H in tobacco and poplar increased frequency of S-units in lignins

perturbations.ev: C4H::F5H transgenic lignin was comprised of as much as 97.5% syringyl units; the syringyl level in the wild-type control was 68%
Line 64 exhibits ca. 94 mol%, line 82 exhibits ca. 85 mol% syringyl lignin (WT 65 mol%)
Ectopic expression of AtF5H cDNA in transgenic tobacco and poplar induces an increase in S-units in lignins
C4H::F5H transformation significantly increased pulping efficiency with decreases of 23 kappa units and increases of >20 ISO brightness
